p.strong: Why both mappings are needed during boot

div.p: The Problem: When CR0.PG is set, the CPU immediately starts translating ALL addresses through page tables. If the code at EIP=0x100000 isn't mapped to virtual 0x100000, the CPU cannot fetch the next instruction → instant page fault → triple fault → reset. The Solution: Identity-map the first 4MB BEFORE enabling paging. This ensures the currently executing code remains accessible after paging is enabled.

ul.li: Simple, but no user space separation
Low addresses free for user processes
Each process gets its own address space
